words: ROB MOORE: Was your grandfather Johah Hayes?
ALICE HENRY JACKSON: Uh huh. His name was Jonah Hayes. Ho was Indian.

speaker: RM
words: Was he a preacher?

speaker: AHJ
words: He was a preacher and he used to preach.

speaker: RM
words: Did you learn some of these songs from him?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, Indian church songs.

speaker: RM
words: Why dont you sing one for me?

speaker: AHJ
words: You gonna catch it?

speaker: RM
words: Yeah.

speaker: AHJ
words: Okay. You ready?

speaker: RM
words: We're ready. Just go ahead.

speaker: AHJ
words: Which one shall I sing for you? (Sings) Is that all right?

speaker: RM
words: Sure.

speaker: AHJ
words: SINGS

speaker: RM
words: Thats very nice. What does that...

speaker: AHJ
words: That is "When the Roll is Called Up Yonder."

speaker: RM
words: What do the words mean?

speaker: AHJ
words: Indian?

speaker: RM
words: Yes.

speaker: AHJ
words: That means when the man comes and sings. The preacher. SINGS.

speaker: RM
words: What does it mean, what have you said so far when you sing that much?

speaker: AHJ
words: When the roll is called up yonder, there will be no more living.

speaker: RM
words: What comes next?

speaker: AHJ
words: Next is, they calling them people across then they call them from there.

speaker: AHJ
words: That's the way it is.

speaker: RM
words: Do you know others?

speaker: AHJ
words: Songs? SINGS. Can you catch that?

speaker: RM
words: What does that one mean?

speaker: AHJ
words: That's Down At the Cross.

speaker: RM
words: Who wrote the Nez Perce wods for these? A lot of these are hymns in English

speaker: RM
words: too, aren't they?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah.

speaker: RM
words: Do you know who wrote the Nez Perce words for them?

speaker: AHJ
words: I don't know. I can't tell you that. See, we used to be Catholic first.

speaker: AHJ
words: But I don't know what kind of a trouble they had so we... I go to Church

speaker: AHJ
words: of God now. Do you know Church of God church?

speaker: RM
words: Yes.

speaker: AHJ
words: That's where I go to now. Uh huh.

speaker: RM
words: When you sued to see Father Cataldo, did he used to sing in Nez Perce too?

speaker: AHJ
words: Oh, just what we learned him.(laughs)But he didn't sing very good. They

speaker: AHJ
words: used to make me start, sing first. That's the way they do down there

speaker: AHJ
words: at the Church of God. They make me sing. Uh huh.

speaker: RM
words: What other songs do you sing?

speaker: AHJ
words: Oh, I got quite a few songs. This is another one.SINGS On the Other Side.

speaker: AHJ
words: That's where we'll be then.

speaker: RM
words: What's that one called?

speaker: AHJ
words: When the Roll is Called Up Yonder.

speaker: RM
words: That's the same one you sang the first time, I think.

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh.

speaker: RM
words: Did they teach you songs like Rock of Ages and things like that in Nez

speaker: RM
words: Perce?

speaker: AHJ
words: No.

speaker: RM
words: What else did they teach you?

speaker: AHJ
words: SINGS

speaker: RM
words: Which one is that?

speaker: AHJ
words: What did they call it new?

speaker: RM
words: What do the words mean in Nez Perce.

speaker: AHJ
words: Jesus Christ.

speaker: RM
words: What does that mean?

speaker: AHJ
words: That means we do good to Jesus Christ. Uh huh.

speaker: RM
words: What's the next line?

speaker: AHJ
words: SINGS, Jesus Christ was our... He was part Nez Perce.

speaker: RM
words: What?

speaker: AHJ
words: He was part Nez Perce.And he made himself go into Mary's stomach. And

speaker: AHJ
words: he was born from there.That song I...

speaker: RM
words: Do you know any songs from the early days of the Nez Perce tribes, songs

speaker: RM
words: tat maybe weren't hymns but were songs that the Nez Perce used to sing?

speaker: AHJ
words: Catholic, they don't sing no Nez Perce songs. I used to be Catholic.

speaker: AHJ
words: When we married, we quit. My husband quit. So we both quit.

speaker: RM
words: What was...

speaker: AHJ
words: His name was Paul Jackson.

speaker: RM
words: Paul Jackson was your husband's name.

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh. He died. My father's name was Len Henry.

speaker: RM
words: Len Henry.

speaker: AHJ
words: And he died. And my mamma's name was Agnes. And my grandmother...

speaker: RM
words: What was her Nez Perce name?

speaker: AHJ
words: Whose?

speaker: RM
words: Your mother's?

speaker: AHJ
words: What was her name? It's been so long.

speaker: RM
words: Yeah. What were the early days like when you were just a child? Was the

speaker: RM
words: tribe on the reservation all the time then?

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh. Used to always have church. Like when I got married with Paul

speaker: AHJ
words: and we come to Catholic Church...But I don't know what got into him, then

speaker: AHJ
words: he quit.

speaker: RM
words: Well, what about when...

speaker: AHJ
words: Then he quit with the Church of God Church.

speaker: RM
words: In the early days with the tribe when your were young, did the men still

speaker: RM
words: do a lot of h unting and did you still go gathering camas and everything?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah. We used to get camas and deers. Go out hunting and have deer meat

speaker: AHJ
words: and have dry deer meat and eat that.

speaker: RM
words: What ikind of housing did you live in then?

speaker: AHJ
words: Now?

speaker: RM
words: No, when you were a little girl.

speaker: AHJ
words: Oh, we had a tipi. When we had that tipi, then my grandfather, my father it

speaker: AHJ
words: was, went and built a house. So he built a house, we built a pretty good

speaker: AHJ
words: house and daddy helped him. See daddy was a white man and he helped him.

speaker: AHJ
words: So when they got through there was upstairs to it. My sons live in that

speaker: AHJ
words: house now.

speaker: RM
words: Where would you go to gather, would you pack up the tipi and everything

speaker: RM
words: when you'd go and gather camas and everything?

speaker: AHJ
words: Long time ago?

speaker: RM
words: Yeah, long time ago.

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, we used to take the tipi with us.

speaker: RM
words: Where would you go?

speaker: AHJ
words: Oh, anyplace up in the mountains, huning or something like that. We used

speaker: AHJ
words: to go. My husband used to be great hunter.

speaker: RM
words: With a rifle?

speaker: AHJ
words: We had a rifle already. I used to dry meat. Oh, that dry meat's good.

speaker: RM
words: How would you dry tbe meat?

speaker: AHJ
words: You just take it and take a knife and cut it. If it's about this size

speaker: AHJ
words: and cut it and open it this way.

speaker: RM
words: Just kind of make little strips out of it?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, not strips, wide ones. About this wide.

speaker: RM
words: About a foot wide or so.

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, about this long and you put it on, we used to dry it on smoke. Uh huh.

speaker: AHJ
words: We used to build a fire underneath.

speaker: RM
words: How long would it take to dry it?

speaker: AHJ
words: Oh, it took not too long to dry it. See fire, smoke.

speaker: RM
words: Did you use to tan the hides?

speaker: AHJ
words: Right. And Mamma used to tan the hides.

speaker: RM
words: Was that very harsd to do?

speaker: AHJ
words: We used to eat that deer meat and she used to tan the hides.

speaker: RM
words: Was that hard, to tan hides?

speaker: AHJ
words: It's hard. See. vou're first qonna soak it, anc you su

speaker: AHJ
words: get the deer meat, you just take it and get aknife and cut it, sew it

speaker: AHJ
words: about this long. Then you take it and soak it. When you get soaking it

speaker: AHJ
words: then you can... well we have some kind of a iron take that...

speaker: RM
words: To scrape the fat with?

speaker: AHJ
words: Scrape it. Yeah. Uh huh. Scrape it this way.

speaker: RM
words: Up and down.

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh. That's the way we used to do with the meat, Imean, skin.

speaker: RM
words: Then what would you do? How would you make it so it was tanned?

speaker: AHJ
words: Well, we used to get some kind of... when they used to hunt like that,

speaker: AHJ
words: we'd get the brains from the deer and we'd mix that brains with alittle

speaker: AHJ
words: water. And then we'd soak it in that.

speaker: RM
words: That tans it?

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh. Then you take it and you, we used to take astick and put it over

speaker: AHJ
words: a pole or something, turn It, dry it up and we go pin it up. And we...

speaker: RM
words: Oh you twist the skin around the stick so it got real tight and all the

speaker: RM
words: water ran out.

speaker: AHJ
words: No. Just a little bit.

speaker: RM
words: You just put it on a stick and let it hang.

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah. St hang down and all the water would go out, you know. And you can

speaker: AHJ
words: tan it quick. I know how to tan it.

speaker: RM
words: Then would you make clothing and things out of it?

speaker: AHJ
words: Well, we used to have clothes. Out of the deer hide. Yeah, they used to

speaker: AHJ
words: make dresses and things out of that.

speaker: RM
words: I imagine they .were pretty nice.

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh. Ue used to use it when ue had uar denees er something. We'd uear

speaker: AHJ
words: it then.

speaker: RM
words: When you had what kind of a dance?

speaker: AHJ
words: War dances.

speaker: RM
words: Did the Nez Perce have war dances?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, they have war dances. They used to have war dances.

speaker: RM
words: Uh huh. What were they like?

speaker: AHJ
words: You mean that dress?

speaker: RM
words: No, the dances.

speaker: AHJ
words: Oh, they used to dance, I don't know how I can tell you.

speaker: RM
words: (Laughs) What kind of songs, did they sing songs at the war dances?

speaker: AHJ
words: See, the war dance, see, go up and down.

speaker: RM
words: Would there be somebody playing a drum or something?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, they played a drum. They play a drum and they war danced.

speaker: RM
words: Was there a song they sang?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah.

speaker: RM
words: Do you know that song?

speaker: AHJ
words: I'm getting too old. I've forgot.

speaker: RM
words: gkay. What other kind ox dances did they have?

speaker: AHJ
words: Well, they used to have Indian dance. Well, people used to send the kid

speaker: AHJ
words: out and themselves too. They'd get some kind of a dream or something frorr

speaker: AHJ
words: something. Some kind of animal or something.And that animal would giv

speaker: AHJ
words: them a song. That's what they used to sing.

speaker: RM
words: Did both the boys and girls go out to have medicine dreams?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah.

speaker: RM
words: Did you do that when you were young, too?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, they sent me up in the mountains

speaker: RM
words: How long did you stay?

speaker: AHJ
words: We went up Upper Bear. That's where they sent me up, just one night.

speaker: RM
words: Were you by yourself?

speaker: AHJ
words: Huhuh. And my song and everything.

speaker: RM
words: What animal or being gave you your song?

speaker: AHJ
words: Well, If it's a deer or any kind of animal, uh huh, if it's a deer or

speaker: AHJ
words: elk or sheep or anything...

speaker: RM
words: What animal gave you yours? Or can't you tell me that?

speaker: AHJ
words: Mine was a deer.

speaker: RM
words: A deer. Do you rmember the song of the deer?

speaker: AHJ
words: Iused to sing it, but Idon't sing it no mere.

speaker: AHJ
words: I used to sing it when we used to have a1 dance. I don't ...

speaker: RM
words: What would happen when you came back from the mountain with your song,

speaker: RM
words: what would you do then?

speaker: AHJ
words: Nothing.

speaker: RM
words: Would there be a dance or something then when you got back?

speaker: AHJ
words: No. Dance at wintertime. We used to dance at wintertime all the time,

speaker: AHJ
words: uh huh. Mamma used to dance and grandma used to dance and grandfather

speaker: AHJ
words: and me. The boys never danced, I had three brothers, and one sister. But

speaker: AHJ
words: she never went anyplace.

speaker: RM
words: Wouid there be big dances with a lot of people there?

speaker: AHJ
words: Y eah. Yeah, we used to have long tent. Built like this, see. Tent poles

speaker: AHJ
words: would be just like that, see. Real long tent.

speaker: RM
words: Real long with the poles locking together.

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh. And we used to have dances in there.

speaker: RM
words: Would there be a lot of people there?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, there used to be lots of people come to dance. You'd see lots of

speaker: AHJ
words: old people, the one's that's old there for some kind of doctoring. Yeah,

speaker: AHJ
words: they doctor a person that was sick. Yeah, grandfather was like that.

speaker: RM
words: How would he do that? What kind of things would he do to doctor somebody?

speaker: AHJ
words: Well they sing. When they're dancing they talk. When they're taking care

speaker: AHJ
words: of the sick people you just sing and you just blow on them.

speaker: RM
words: Blow on the person?

speaker: AHJ
words: UH huh. Blow on a person.

speaker: RM
words: Would they use plants and things too, to help doctor with?

speaker: AHJ
words: No. They just(makes blowing sound) like that.

speaker: RM
words: How did that help?

speaker: AHJ
words: They used to help them. See first he, when he's going to doctor then he'd

speaker: AHJ
words: talk, talk his dream, what he dreamt about, them songs. Then he'd talk

speaker: AHJ
words: about that and when he'd get through, then he can blow onto them.

speaker: RM
words: I see. And he would get his pouier through that dream?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah. Then he can take your head this way. He'd go up, hold you up.They

speaker: AHJ
words: used to, my grandfather was like that. He used to doctor and he was to

speaker: AHJ
words: doctor even pretty sick people. He'd get them well. That's the way it

speaker: AHJ
words: it.

speaker: RM
words: What did, when you were a girl with the Nez Perce, did the tribe move

speaker: RM
words: around quite a bit?

speaker: AHJ
words: We used to go out in the mountains. Get deer meat, kill deer and we'd

speaker: AHJ
words: dry it. We used to have dry meat.

speaker: RM
words: Whet would you eat all winter, dry meat and what else?

speaker: RM
words: AB3: Yeah, you could eat dry meat or else the boys would go out and hunt

speaker: RM
words: again, uh huh. Dear meat's pretty good. Have you ever eaten it?

speaker: RM
words: Yeah.

speaker: AHJ
words: Deer meat's pretty good.

speaker: RM
words: Would you eat the camas too?

speaker: AHJ
words: Camas, cous.

speaker: RM
words: Cous?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah. They was different. Coub is different. When you cook camas it turns

speaker: AHJ
words: black. When they cook cous they don't cook it under the ground, but this

speaker: AHJ
words: other one they could cook it under the ground. This other one they don't,

speaker: AHJ
words: they just dry It up. Just take the top off and wash it clean and dry it

speaker: AHJ
words: up.That's cous.

speaker: RM
words: What does cous look like?

speaker: AHJ
words: White. They're white and...

speaker: RM
words: Did they have a white flower?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, kind of a pret'near yellow. They grow all over the hillsides.

speaker: RM
words: How would you dig camas?

speaker: AHJ
words: Oh we used to have a digger,

speaker: RM
words: What did it look like?

speaker: AHJ
words: Toucas we called it. About this long, Iron.

speaker: RM
words: What did you call it?

speaker: AHJ
words: Toucas. And it's just about like this.

speaker: RM
words: A little bit of a bend.

speaker: AHJ
words: My mamma used to put a piece of wood, clean It good and put that under

speaker: AHJ
words: it like this.

speaker: RM
words: On the end of the toucas.

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah. See, they used to make a hole in that thing and put it on the

speaker: AHJ
words: tourcas.

speaker: RM
words: I see. Then)you just dig that under the plants.

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, and dig the cous out and camas.

speaker: RM
words: How would you make the camas so it wouldn't spoil?

speaker: AHJ
words: Well, its just the same, but see, we have to cook it first. We used to

speaker: AHJ
words: do that, cooks used to do that. Mamma. They dig up ground.

speaker: RM
words: Dig a big hole in the ground?

speaker: AHJ
words: Dig a big hole in the ground, oh, about this deep.

speaker: RM
words: Couple, three feet deep.

speaker: AHJ
words: They put wood on top of it like this.

speaker: RM
words: Uh huh. Overlapping pieces of wood.

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, Then they put rocks on top of It, on top, then they put in that.

speaker: RM
words: The camas would be under the wood?

speaker: AHJ
words: No, it's gonna be on top. And then whbn they burn that wood they fall,

speaker: AHJ
words: you know. They take all pieces of wood out and just have hot rocks. They'd

speaker: AHJ
words: take rocks and put it on top. And that rocks get hot, they fall in, then

speaker: AHJ
words: they take grass and they kind of sprinkle the rock. And they put the grass

speaker: AHJ
words: on top of it. And they put the camas, that's camas, it turn black. They

speaker: AHJ
words: take it... mamma used to do it, put it in a gunny sack and put it on this

speaker: AHJ
words: way.

speaker: RM
words: What do you mean this way?

speaker: AHJ
words: This way, stand like this. Like this, see.

speaker: RM
words: The gunny sacks would stand up against each other.

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah.

speaker: RM
words: I see.

speaker: AHJ
words: Then she'd burn it then. And it'll get ripe.

speaker: RM
words: Then how, what do you do to eat it?

speaker: AHJ
words: To eat it? Well we take it out and we take it and dry it out. Mamma used

speaker: AHJ
words: to take the ones that's small and she'd take that and pound it. To make

speaker: AHJ
words: soup out of it. Sure good soup.

speaker: RM
words: I've never eaten camas.

speaker: AHJ
words: You never eat camas?

speaker: RM
words: No.

speaker: AHJ
words: Oh, you never eat eous too?

speaker: RM
words: No. How would you prepare cous?

speaker: AHJ
words: Well you have to dry it on the sun. My mamma used to take it and put it

speaker: AHJ
words: on top of the house like this. Just a cloth or something and put it on

speaker: AHJ
words: that. And dry it up. When it's dry it's good to eat.

speaker: RM
words: Just like that?

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh. If you want to cook it, you can cook It, but if you want to eat

speaker: AHJ
words: it dry, it's dry.

speaker: RM
words: How would you cook it if you wanted to cook it?

speaker: AHJ
words: Boil it, boil it and you can put little greaso or something in it. And

speaker: AHJ
words: it tastes good. Same way with camas. Camas is black, when they get ripe

speaker: AHJ
words: they turn black.

speaker: RM
words: They're black all the way through.

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh. We used to take it and pound some of it. They take it, they make

speaker: AHJ
words: it, oh, about this long.

speaker: RM
words: About a foot long or so.

speaker: AJ
words: About this long. Dry it up like that. It used to be good to eat.

speaker: RM
words: When you were traveling along, would you eat dry meat and camas and

speaker: RM
words: things like that?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah.

speaker: RM
words: How did you pull the tipi and things, how did you move all that stuff

speaker: RM
words: when you moved somewhere?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, we used to take our tipi with us.

speaker: RM
words: How would you do it?

speaker: AHJ
words: Pack it.

speaker: RM
words: On a horse?

speaker: AHJ
words: On a horse. We used to go from here clean to Wallowa and then coming back

speaker: AHJ
words: we'd go up in the mountain. And that's where the boys used to hunt.

speaker: RM
words: Up on Moscow Mountain, what they call Moscow Mountain?

speaker: AHJ
words: On this side of Wallowa. On this side of Wallowa, that's where it was.

speaker: AHJ
words: And we used to come clear down to Lapwai when we get home. That's how

speaker: AHJ
words: we used to do it. We used to get salmon in Wallowa. Salmon, we used to

speaker: AHJ
words: get salmon. We used to dip it with a dipnet.

speaker: RM
words: Did you smoke the salmon or dry the salmon?

speaker: AHJ
words: We just dry it. Not smoke it, but dry it. It's good if you eat it dry.

speaker: AHJ
words: It's alright. If you cook it, it's alright. Uh huh. We used to eat eels

speaker: AHJ
words: too.

speaker: RM
words: From where? Did you get them out of Wallowa Lake?

speaker: AHJ
words: We used to get eels and dry it just the same. We have to have sticks to

speaker: AHJ
words: string them up. And dry good. Just put it across like this. And dry it.

speaker: AHJ
words: If its in the fall time, then we used to put fire under it. Mamma used

speaker: AHJ
words: to do that. In the summertime you can dry it on the sun, with the sun.

speaker: AHJ
words: It's good. I used to like it.

speaker: RM
words: How much time would you spend down In Wallowa each year?

speaker: AHJ
words: We used to after July, we'd go over there and stay over there about t©o

speaker: AHJ
words: weeks then we'd come back.

speaker: RM
words: In two weeks you managed to get enough fish and everything?

speaker: AHJ
words: Folks used to. My grandfather used to get dipnet. Dip it with a dipnet,

speaker: AHJ
words: salmon. And we used to have dried salmon.

speaker: RM
words: How many people were there in those times? How many Nez Perce were

speaker: RM
words: there when you were making those big moves down from Wallowa and back to

speaker: RM
words: here when you were moving,how big a tribe would be moving?

speaker: AHJ
words: There was lots of people that used to go to Wallowa.

speaker: RM
words: Would you all go in one bunch?

speaker: RM
words: No, some went up on the other side and some down. We used to be down

speaker: RM
words: all the time. We used to get salmon about this long,

speaker: RM
words: Those are big fish, a couple feet long.

speaker: AHJ
words: Uh huh. We used to get, they used to even dry salmon. You can eat it dry.

speaker: AHJ
words: It's good. You don't have to cook it, but sometimes this backbone and the

speaker: AHJ
words: head.

speaker: AHJ
words: We had no tipi for that. We just used to go over there and camp.

speaker: RM
words: Do you remember who any of the elders or old people were who...

speaker: AHJ
words: Grandma, grandfather, and my grandmother.

speaker: RM
words: What were their names?

speaker: AHJ
words: Jonah Hayes, my father's grandfather's name was. And my grandmother's

speaker: AHJ
words: was Elizabeth.

speaker: RM
words: This was all after the war, wasn't it?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah. It was after the war. After they had this war that they had.

speaker: RM
words: What did the Nez Perce say about that war?

speaker: AHJ
words: Well, I don't know(chuckles). I don't know very much about it.

speaker: RM
words: Uh huh. W9re the Nez Perce sorry that it happened or...

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, they were sorry it happened that way. See, all kinds of, I don't

speaker: AHJ
words: know. I don't know what they call it. Snake Indian, I guess. They're the

speaker: AHJ
words: ones that used to come and fight the Nez Perce.

speaker: RM
words: What would the fights be about?

speaker: AHJ
words: Well, I don't know, just try to take the land away from us. I think that's

speaker: AHJ
words: what It was. Take the land away.

speaker: RM
words: The Nez Perce were really good horsemen weren't they?

speaker: AHJ
words: Yeah, uh huh. They?re pretty good. We used to dry salmon, dry meat. Dry

speaker: AHJ
words: meat is awful good. And mamma used to take me when we dried and she'd
